name: OWASP ZAP (Web Application Security Scanner)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); OWASP / Software Security Project

auth.notes: ZAP API key (set in ZAP or via -config api.key=xxx)

performance.notes: Scan speed depends on target app complexity; active scan can take hours

agent_readiness.notes: OWASP's open-source DAST (Dynamic Application Security Testing) tool. Python ZAP API client. Spider, AJAX Spider, Active Scan, Passive Scan. zap.spider.scan(target). zap.ascan.scan(target). zap.core.alerts(). Jenkins/GitHub Actions integration. Docker container for CI/CD DAST. HUD (browser-integrated). Session-based scanning. Authentication support (form/API). OWASP Top 10 coverage. ZAP Automation Framework. The standard free DAST tool for web apps.

gotchas: Active scans can be slow and noisy on target app
Only scan authorized targets — unauthorized scanning is illegal
Requires ZAP running locally or in container (not pure Python)
best_when: DAST in CI/CD pipelines for authorized web applications
Security regression testing for web APIs
Penetration testing web apps with proxy interception
avoid_when: Source code analysis (use Semgrep/Bandit for SAST)
summary: OWASP ZAP is the standard open-source DAST tool. Python API client controls spider, active/passive scans, alert retrieval. Docker container for CI/CD. OWASP Top 10 coverage. ZAP Automation Framework for scriptable scanning. Session-based with authentication support. Only scan authorized targets. Free alternative to Burp Suite for automated DAST in pipelines.
